metadata.title: liver (v1.4) graph data
metadata.description: The graph representation of the Anatomical Structures, Cell Types, plus Biomarkers (ASCT+B) table for Liver dataset.

cell_types.ccf_pref_label: columnar cell (larger cholangiocytes are more columnar) has ciliae

anatomical_structure_list.label: liver (Table liver, Record 1, Column AS/1)
hepatic Lobule (Table liver, Record 1, Column AS/2)
hepatic sinusoid (Table liver, Record 1, Column AS/3)

cell_type_list.label: liver sinusoid endothelial cell (Table liver, Record 1, Column CT/1)

ftu_list.notes: Liver lobus are 1-2 mm in diameter and 2 mm long and the liver has around 100000 lobules he lobule contain the capillaries called hepatic sinusoids. The hepatic sinusoids are branching from hepatic artery (part of the triad ) and converge to central vein. Around the sinusoids are the sinusoid endothelial cells those form a fenestrated endothleium. 2/3 rd of the lobule are made up by hepatocytes
ftu_list.label: hepatic Lobule (Table liver, Record 1, Column FTU/1)

anatomical_structure_list.label: liver (Table liver, Record 2, Column AS/1)
hepatic Lobule (Table liver, Record 2, Column AS/2)
hepatic sinusoid (Table liver, Record 2, Column AS/3)

cell_type_list.label: Kupffer cell (Table liver, Record 2, Column CT/1)

ftu_list.notes: The fluid in addition to blood in the capillaries there is a tissue fluid goes from the sinusoids into the perisinusoidal space (Disse space). this fluid is main used by hepathocytes that present microvilli that extend into the Disse space to allow more product to be absorbed. it is a circle secretion and absorbtion of fluid from capillaries and hepathocytes
ftu_list.label: hepatic Lobule (Table liver, Record 2, Column FTU/1)

anatomical_structure_list.label: liver (Table liver, Record 3, Column AS/1)
hepatic Lobule (Table liver, Record 3, Column AS/2)
Disse's space (Table liver, Record 3, Column AS/3)

cell_type_list.label: quiescent hepatic stellate cell (Table liver, Record 3, Column CT/1)

ftu_list.notes: one of the function of the liver unit is to filter the blood coming from the digestive tract and before going to the rest of the body. It is the location for metabolization of drugsor alchool. is the manifacture of the coagulation processes. Drug induce Liver injury is one of the main event due to drug toxicity .
ftu_list.label: hepatic Lobule (Table liver, Record 3, Column FTU/1)
